candidates.studentStem: A:resurs80->54, miljö70->76, arter18->24. B:resurs80->83, miljö70->76, arter18->24. C:resurs80->54, miljö70->52, arter18->12. D:resurs80->83, miljö70->52, arter18->12. Vilken slutsats följer av C-raden?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: C combines resource 80 to 54 with habitat 70 to 52 and species 18 to 12, so it is a resource gain with biological cost. A is resource gain with biological gain, B is resource cost with biological gain and D is resource cost with biological cost. (b) Distractor types: A is Type I positive-biology shortcut, B is Type II biology-only shortcut, D is Type III resource-direction mismatch. (c) Cognitive dimensions: two-direction comparison, habitat interpretation, species interpretation and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests trade-off classification in sustainable production. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus habitat/species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: neutral option labels avoid keyword echo.

shortcut_check.reviewerNote: Pure text format is allowed here because the stem contains compact scenario or numeric data; the answer requires multiple inferences.

candidates.studentStem: A: vara1 resurs 54 arter 24; vara2 resurs 70 arter 19. B: vara1 resurs 70 arter 24; vara2 resurs 54 arter 19. C: vara1 resurs 54 arter 19; vara2 resurs 70 arter 24. D: vara1 resurs 70 arter 19; vara2 resurs 54 arter 24. Vilken rad visar lägst resursvärde och flest arter för vara1?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A gives product one resource 54 versus product two resource 70 and product one species 24 versus product two species 19. B keeps the species advantage but reverses resource direction, C keeps resource direction but reverses species direction, and D reverses both. Correct A is the only row with lower resource value and higher species value for product one. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I species-only shortcut, C is Type II resource-only shortcut, D is Type III double-reversal. (c) Cognitive dimensions: paired data comparison, resource-pressure reading, species-outcome reading and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests biological product comparison from two measures. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: neutral row labels and separated numeric values create explicit data load without keyword echo.

candidates.studentStem: A:T+0,7/20 före, blomstart42->35, pollinering saknas. B:T+0,7/20 efter, blomstart42->35, pollinering58->49. C:T+0,7/20 före, blomstart42->35, pollinering58->49. D:T+0,7/2 före, blomstart42->35, pollinering58->49. Vilken orsakskedja håller?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: C has T plus 0.7 over 20 years before flower start changes 42 to 35 and pollination changes 58 to 49. A lacks pollination, B places warming after the biological changes and D uses only two years. Correct C is the supported causal chain. (b) Distractor types: A is Type I missing-outcome shortcut, B is Type II reversed-order shortcut, D is Type III short-window shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: time-order reasoning, duration reasoning, phenology interpretation and function-outcome inference. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests a climate-to-biological-function chain. (e) Knowledge link map: long warming -> timing -> pollination. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: neutral option labels remove keyword echo.
